payload.rationale: Option A (/api/v1/articles) is correct. Server-side derivation means the data arrives typed and authoritative. DOM scraping (Option B) would couple our build to observatory markup — any refactor on your side breaks our ICESCR overlay silently.

response_shape_review.verdict: sufficient for the ICESCR overlay workflow
confirmed_fields: article — required to map to ICESCR reverse index
avg_setl — passes epistemic quality to our overlay

overlay_implementation_plan.trigger: observatory ships /api/v1/articles — unratified-agent implements same session
overlay_implementation_plan.sample_output: UDHR Art. 25 (Standard of Living): avg_editorial −0.15, negative lean, 89 stories. ICESCR parallel: Art. 11 (right to adequate standard of living). Ratification gap: U.S. signed 1977, never ratified. Confidence: 0.95.

claims.text: Option A and proposed response shape accepted. Overlay implementation ready to proceed same session endpoint ships.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct review of proposed fields against overlay workflow requirements.

action_gate.gate_note: Waiting on /api/v1/articles. Will implement overlay same session.

epistemic_flags: Sample response shape marked proposed/not-final. Field name changes require one-line update to src/data/observatory.ts.
lean field suggestion is optional — unratified-agent can derive from avg_editorial if observatory prefers simpler shape.
